@tailwind.description: Directive to inject Tailwind's generated styles into your CSS. Each layer can be included independently.

@apply.description: Inline existing Tailwind utility classes into custom CSS rules. Enables reusing utility-first patterns in component styles.

@layer.description: Tells Tailwind which layer a set of custom styles belongs to. Valid layers: base, components, utilities.

@config.description: Specifies which Tailwind config file to use for a CSS file. Useful in monorepos with multiple configs.

theme().description: Access Tailwind theme values in CSS. E.g. `theme('colors.blue.500')` returns `#3b82f6`. Supports dot notation for nested values.

screen().description: Reference responsive breakpoints by name in media queries. E.g. `@media screen('md')` compiles to `@media (min-width: 768px)`.

container.description: A component class that sets width: 100% and applies responsive max-width at each breakpoint (sm: 640px, md: 768px, lg: 1024px, xl: 1280px, 2xl: 1536px).

sm.description: Responsive prefix applying styles at min-width: 640px. Mobile-first breakpoint.

md.description: Responsive prefix applying styles at min-width: 768px.

lg.description: Responsive prefix applying styles at min-width: 1024px.

xl.description: Responsive prefix applying styles at min-width: 1280px.

2xl.description: Responsive prefix applying styles at min-width: 1536px.

arbitrary-values.description: Use arbitrary values with square bracket notation for one-off styles. E.g. `w-[200px]`, `bg-[#1da1f2]`, `grid-cols-[1fr_2fr]`.

important-modifier.description: Prefix any utility with `!` to generate the style with `!important`. E.g. `!p-4` → `padding: 1rem !important`.

dark-mode.description: Applies styles when dark mode is active. Controlled by `darkMode` option in config: 'media' (prefers-color-scheme) or 'class' (.dark parent).

group.description: Mark a parent with `group` class and style children with `group-{state}:` variants. Children respond to parent hover/focus/active states.

peer.description: Mark a sibling with `peer` class and use `peer-{state}:` on subsequent siblings. Enables sibling-based conditional styling.

jit-mode.description: Just-In-Time compilation mode generates styles on demand. Default in Tailwind CSS v3+. Enables arbitrary values, all variants, and faster build times.

utility-class.description: Tailwind utility classes follow a `{property}-{value}` pattern. The property maps to a CSS property and the value maps to a design token from the theme configuration.

negative-values.description: Prefix utilities with `-` for negative values. E.g. `-m-4` → `margin: -1rem`, `-translate-x-full` → `transform: translateX(-100%)`.

tailwind-config.description: Configuration file for customizing Tailwind: theme (colors, spacing, fonts), plugins, content paths, dark mode, prefix, important, and variant order.

content-configuration.description: Specifies file paths for Tailwind to scan for class names. Required for tree-shaking unused styles. Supports glob patterns.
